topic: special bench to fast-track rape-murder cases
headline_marked: [[Special legal team]] formed to fast-track death-reference cases in High Court
headline: Special legal team formed to fast-track death-reference cases in High Court
summary: A dedicated team, led by the Attorney General, will represent the state in High Court hearings for death-reference cases under the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act, aiming for swift justice.
details: A special legal team, headed by Attorney General Barrister Md Ruhul Quddus Kazal, has been established to represent the state in the High Court for death-reference cases concerning the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act. This includes high-profile cases such as the Ramisa rape and murder.

The team comprises Deputy Attorneys General Osman Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan, and Jamiul Haque Faisal, along with Assistant Attorney General Dewan Humayun Kabir Ripon. The Attorney General has stated that the state will not seek adjournments during the hearings for the death reference and appeal in the Ramisa case.

A death reference is a mandatory judicial procedure where a trial court's death sentence is sent to the High Court for confirmation before execution. Convicts also have the right to appeal their sentences.

The High Court will hear the death reference and appeal for the Ramisa rape and murder case once the paper-book is prepared. The Bangladesh Government Press is expediting the printing of the paper-book, which contains all lower court judgments, orders, statements, and evidence necessary for the High Court's review.

A special High Court bench, formed by Chief Justice Zubayer Rahman Chowdhury, has been designated to exclusively hear and dispose of death sentence cases under the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act. This bench will prioritize death reference cases and also handle related criminal appeals and revision petitions.
caption:
Justice expedited: A special legal team is now in place to fast-track death-reference cases, including the Ramisa rape and murder, in the High Court.

A special legal team, headed by Attorney General Barrister Md Ruhul Quddus Kazal, has been established to represent the state in the High Court for death-reference cases concerning the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act. This includes high-profile cases such as the Ramisa rape and murder.

The team comprises Deputy Attorneys General Osman Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan, and Jamiul Haque Faisal, along with Assistant Attorney General Dewan Humayun Kabir Ripon. The Attorney General has stated that the state will not seek adjournments during the hearings for the death reference and appeal in the Ramisa case.

A death reference is a mandatory judicial procedure where a trial court's death sentence is sent to the High Court for confirmation before execution. Convicts also have the right to appeal their sentences.

The High Court will hear the death reference and appeal for the Ramisa rape and murder case once the paper-book is prepared. The Bangladesh Government Press is expediting the printing of the paper-book, which contains all lower court judgments, orders, statements, and evidence necessary for the High Court's review.

A special High Court bench, formed by Chief Justice Zubayer Rahman Chowdhury, has been designated to exclusively hear and dispose of death sentence cases under the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act. This bench will prioritize death reference cases and also handle related criminal appeals and revision petitions.

#Bangladesh #Law #Justice #HighCourt #WomenAndChildrenRepressionPreventionAct

Source: Dhaka Tribune, The Daily Star, Jamuna TV
